social_media_sentiment: TikTok clip of Amjad Advocate got 563 likes/26 comments — solid organic PPP engagement. PTI-backed accounts attacking him as 'establishment-friendly' paradoxically mobilizes PPP base. Bilawal's GB visit amplified his posts.
ground_reality: Azar has 5-year incumbency + UC-level patronage + PPP national leadership personally campaigning. PTI's MWM proxy (Siddiqi) lacks ballot recognition. PML-N's Shafiquddin is a placeholder. PPP's ground tents (shumaliyati) outnumber PML-N in Gilgit-I.

social_media_sentiment: High PTI base energy on X — @PTIGBOfficial posts about 'state tricks' going viral among youth. MWM symbol confuses Sunni voters. TikTok PTI content strong in Gilgit city, weak in rural wards.
ground_reality: PTI's persecution narrative ('state using every trick to block us') is paradoxically mobilizing the base MORE than 2020. But without bat symbol, rural voters in Juglot/Jutial/Oshikhandas won't find him on ballot. Siddiqi is NOT Khalid Khurshid — lacks CM-level name recognition.

social_media_sentiment: Negligible digital footprint. PML-N GB social media focused on Hafeezur Rehman (GBA-2), not GBA-1.
ground_reality: PML-N has NEVER won GBA-1 in recent history. Shafiquddin is a vote-splitter to hurt PTI, not to win. PML-N's 'federal advantage' is overstated — they're struggling to find electables per Leopard Media analysis.

social_media_sentiment: Maryam Nawaz GB visit videos went viral. PML-N machinery posts strong. But TikTok shows PTI youth still highly engaged — 'Halka 2' video got 738 likes/82 comments showing competitive race.
ground_reality: Hafeezur Rehman is PML-N GB President + former CM + Nawaz Sharif personally interviewed him. BUT — Leopard Media reports PML-N 'struggling to secure strong electables.' Fatehullah Khan (IPP) splits PTI vote, which actually HELPS Hafeezur. Without IPP, Pirzada (Ind/PTI) might win.

social_media_sentiment: High TikTok engagement for PTI-backed content. Youth mobilization visible. 'Next Leader of GB: PPP, PTI, or PML-N?' TikTok got 1,596 likes — PTI comments dominant.
ground_reality: GBA-2 was PTI's closest 2020 win (4 votes). Pirzada lacks Fatehullah's name recognition but PTI's persecution narrative is energizing base. Without bat symbol, older voters confused. IPP's Fatehullah peels 3,000 PTI votes — this is the ONLY reason PML-N wins.

social_media_sentiment: Minimal IPP digital presence. Seen as 'Gulbar Khan ally' and 'turncoat' by PTI loyalists. Comments on PTI pages attack him viciously.
ground_reality: IPP is the 'forward bloc' — PTI defectors who joined CM Gulbar Khan. Fatehullah won 2020 on PTI's bat symbol, NOT personal vote. His candidacy helps PML-N by splitting anti-PML-N vote. PTI base sees him as traitor.

social_media_sentiment: PPP Gilgit-III campaign gaining traction after Bilawal's visit. Youth wings active. Haider is new face but getting establishment support posts.
ground_reality: CORRECTION from initial model: Sohail Abbas was 2020 winner as PTI but his personal vote is NOT as sticky as assumed. PPP's Aftab Haider has been campaigning since early 2025 and built ground organization. Bilawal-Aseefa roadshow gave him visibility. Gilgit-III is less federal-alignment-driven than Diamer.

social_media_sentiment: Very high PTI engagement. Abbas was 2020 winner; name recognition strong. But 'where is bat symbol?' confusion visible in comments.
ground_reality: Abbas won 2020 as PTI with strong margin. But 6 years later, voters remember the symbol, not the man. His independent status means no party worker network. PPP's Haider has UC-level coordinators + Bilawal's photo on posters. True toss-up but PPP's organization edges it.

social_media_sentiment: Low PML-N digital activity. Dr Iqbal has professional reputation but limited political base.
ground_reality: PML-N treats GBA-3 as low priority. Dr Iqbal is not competitive — his role is to ensure PML-N presence and peel anti-PPP votes, but Gilgit-III voters are not PML-N-aligned.

social_media_sentiment: PPP traditional base steady in Nagar. Older voters share pro-Bhutto content. Less viral but reliable turnout. Shia-majority pockets historically PPP-leaning.
ground_reality: CORRECTION: Nagar-I was PPP-leaning before 2020 PTI wave. Without PTI bat symbol, the seat reverts to mean. Bheshti (Ind/PTI) has local roots but PTI's 2020 vote was 60% symbol-driven. Muhammad Ali Akhter's family political legacy + PPP's Shia outreach still resonates. This is <800 vote margin.

social_media_sentiment: PTI youth networks active in Nagar. Bheshti has local roots. High share/comment ratio on PTI-aligned posts.
ground_reality: Bheshti benefits from PTI's 2020 flip but that vote was bat-symbol-driven. Without it, he's fighting uphill. His personal vote is maybe 40% of his 2020 total. PML-N's Imtiaz Hussain peels another 2,500 votes, helping PPP.

social_media_sentiment: Negligible PML-N social media presence in Nagar.
ground_reality: PML-N has minimal organizational footprint in Nagar. Imtiaz Hussain is a vote-splitter to hurt PTI, not to win. His 2,500 votes come from government employees and business community.

social_media_sentiment: Manwa's switch from Independent to PML-N created buzz. PML-N machinery backing him. But TikTok shows PPP youth engagement rising in Nagar-II.
ground_reality: Manwa won 2020 as Independent with strong personal vote. PML-N ticket adds federal resources BUT — Leopard Media reports PML-N struggling with electables. Manwa is one of their FEW strong candidates. PPP's Zulfiqar Murad has been campaigning hard and closing gap. MWM/PTI's Riaz Akbar peels Shia votes, helping Manwa.

social_media_sentiment: PPP network activity rising. Murad is known and getting Bilawal visit boost. Standard but growing engagement.
ground_reality: PPP lost Nagar-II in 2020 but Murad is rebuilding. Bilawal's Nagar visit (if it happened) would give him boost. The gap is closing but Manwa's personal vote + PML-N resources still enough. Not a safe seat anymore.

social_media_sentiment: MWM networks active in Shia pockets of Nagar. PTI base confused about MWM alliance but religious voters recognize umbrella symbol.
ground_reality: Riaz Akbar is PTI's MWM proxy. MWM has genuine Shia base in Nagar-II. His 4,000 votes come from religious mobilization, not PTI loyalty. These votes would have gone to PPP in past elections — his presence helps PML-N.

social_media_sentiment: PPP gaining traction among progressive Hunza voters. AWP/leftist social media indirectly boosting PPP. Col Imtiaz's military background posts get respect shares.
ground_reality: Hunza is GB's most politically conscious district. AWP not contesting prominently — their voters drifting to PPP as 'lesser evil.' Prince Saleem's 'royal family' narrative alienates educated youth. PTI's Kareem is unknown. PPP's progressive messaging + Col Imtiaz's establishment credentials = winning combination in Hunza.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Hunza campaign visible but 'royal family' narrative mocked by younger voters on TikTok. Conservative voters share his content.
ground_reality: Prince Saleem (Shahzada Saleem) represents old Hunza royal lineage. Matters for conservative voters over 50 but alienates under-35 progressives who are 40% of electorate. PML-N's federal alignment helps with business community but Hunza voters prioritize education/health/roads over federal patronage.

social_media_sentiment: PTI Hunza support fragmented. Kareem is not well-known digitally. Low engagement.
ground_reality: Kareem is PTI's proxy but Hunza-I had independent winner in 2020 — not PTI. PTI's bat ban hurts more in literate Hunza where voters know symbols. Kareem has no independent brand. PTI's 2020 Hunza vote was anti-royalty, not pro-PTI.

social_media_sentiment: Very high engagement for Syed family content. 'Syed Mehdi Shah legacy' posts widely shared. Emotional connection + religious reverence. Bilawal's Skardu visit amplified this massively.
ground_reality: Skardu-I is the Syed family's FORTRESS. Syed Mehdi Shah (father) won 2009, 2015; came 3rd in 2020 ONLY due to PTI national wave. Tauqeer is his son — massive biraderi + emotional + religious vote. Raja Zakaria Maqpoon (Ind/PTI) won 2020 on PTI wave but has ZERO personal vote. PTI's persecution narrative doesn't work in Baltistan where voters remember 2020 as 'Imran's magic, not PTI's organization.'

social_media_sentiment: PTI Skardu base vocal online but Maqpoon lacks independent charisma. Comments mock him as 'one-hit wonder.'
ground_reality: Maqpoon was a SURPRISE 2020 winner on PTI's bat symbol. Without it, he's exposed. His Raja biraderi is small in Skardu-I. PTI's Baltistan vote bank was always SOFT — driven by Imran Khan's national popularity, not local organization. 2020 was an anomaly; 2026 reverts to mean.

social_media_sentiment: Low PML-N digital presence in Skardu. Taban is known locally but not a social media figure.
ground_reality: PML-N has minimal Baltistan organizational structure. Taban is a respectable figure but cannot overcome the Syed family juggernaut. PML-N treats Skardu-I as unwinnable — their candidate is symbolic.

social_media_sentiment: MWM Skardu networks highly organized digitally. Shia religious sentiment mobilized. Maisam's 2020 win created strong brand. Umbrella symbol is recognizable.
ground_reality: GBA-8 is MWM's STRONGEST seat in all GB. Shia-majority, religiously organized, and Maisam is incumbent. PTI's backing adds youth energy but MWM's own symbol (umbrella) is recognizable — this is KEY difference from other PTI-proxy seats. One of the safest PTI-proxy seats.

social_media_sentiment: PPP standard activity. Syed name helps but MWM's religious mobilization is stronger here.
ground_reality: PPP has tried to compete in Skardu-II through Syed family connections but MWM's sectarian-based organization is unmatched. Shah is running to keep PPP visible, not to win.

social_media_sentiment: Minimal PML-N activity. Haider has some local name but no digital campaign.
ground_reality: PML-N is not competitive in Skardu-II. Haider's candidacy is symbolic. The real contest is MWM vs PPP.

social_media_sentiment: JUI-F religious networks active. Saleem's 2020 win as Independent gives him personal brand. Moderate engagement. Religious sermons shared on WhatsApp.
ground_reality: Saleem won GBA-9 in 2020 as Independent with strong personal vote. Now with JUI-F ticket, he gets religious network support too. But Fida Nashad (PPP) was 2020 runner-up as PTI — his switch to PPP gives him party machinery. Wazir Azher Mehdi (MWM/PTI) peels PTI votes, helping Saleem.

social_media_sentiment: Mixed sentiment. Nashad's PTI-to-PPP switch criticized online as 'lotacracy' by PTI loyalists. But PPP machinery backing him and Bilawal's visit gave him photo-ops.
ground_reality: Nashad's party switch is his weakness AND strength. PTI loyalists see him as traitor; PPP voters see him as 'returning to Bhutto fold.' He's caught in no-man's land but PPP's organization is carrying him. Without MWM/PTI spoiler, he might win.

social_media_sentiment: MWM religious content shared in Skardu-III. PTI base partially mobilized but confused by MWM alliance.
ground_reality: Mehdi is PTI's MWM proxy in Skardu-III. MWM has some base here but not as strong as GBA-8. He'll draw enough votes to ensure Saleem's win by splitting PPP's vote. PTI's persecution narrative doesn't work as well in religious Skardu-III.

social_media_sentiment: High engagement for Maqpoon. His 2020 win as Independent + PPP ticket now = strong narrative. 'Raja' biraderi mobilized. Bilawal's Skardu roadshow featured him prominently.
ground_reality: Maqpoon is incumbent who won GBA-10 in 2020 as Independent. Now with PPP ticket, he has BOTH personal vote + party organization. Dr Shareef (Ind/PTI) is PTI's proxy but Maqpoon already absorbed the independent vote in 2020. PML-N's Hassan is non-factor.

social_media_sentiment: PTI Skardu-IV base active but Shareef is not known political figure. Digital campaign struggling. 'Where is bat symbol?' confusion.
ground_reality: Shareef is PTI's medical professional candidate. Competent but lacks political charisma. In Skardu-IV, biraderi politics (Maqpoon's Raja network) trumps professional credentials. PTI's 2020 vote here was anti-status-quo, not pro-Shareef.

social_media_sentiment: Low PML-N digital presence. Hassan has some local support but not competitive.
ground_reality: PML-N treats Skardu-IV as low priority. Hassan's role is to ensure PML-N gets some votes and potentially split anti-PPP vote, but Maqpoon's dominance is too strong.

social_media_sentiment: PPP Kharmang network steady. Hassan has local roots. Moderate engagement. Bilawal's visit to Kharmang (if occurred) would boost.
ground_reality: Kharmang is PPP-leaning. Iqbal Hassan was runner-up in 2020 as Independent; now PPP ticket gives him organizational backing. Syed Mohsin Rizvi (PML-N) is competent but PML-N historically weak in Kharmang. Syed Mohsin Zaidi (Ind/PTI) gets residual PTI vote but Kharmang voters prioritize local patronage.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Kharmang campaign low-key. Rizvi has some Syed family connections but not dominant.
ground_reality: Rizvi is PML-N's attempt to build in Kharmang but the district has historically resisted PML-N. Federal alignment helps marginally but Kharmang's isolation means local candidates win.

social_media_sentiment: PTI Kharmang support fragmented. Zaidi not prominent online.
ground_reality: Zaidi is PTI's proxy but Kharmang's PTI vote was soft in 2020. Without bat symbol, difficult to mobilize. Kharmang voters prioritize local development over national party loyalty.

social_media_sentiment: PPP Shigar campaign active and growing. Nadeem getting youth wing support. TikTok shows Shigar youth more engaged with PPP than expected.
ground_reality: CORRECTION from initial model: Shigar is biraderi-driven but NOT PML-N territory. Tahir Shigri's family name matters but PPP's Imran Nadeem has been campaigning for 18 months and built strong UC network. Raja Azam Khan Amacha (TLP) won 2020 as PTI but TLP is irrelevant in GB — his 2020 vote was bat-symbol, not personal. Nadeem benefits from TLP vote collapse + PPP national campaign push. This is a genuine toss-up.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Shigar campaign visible. Shigri biraderi mobilized. But engagement lower than PPP's youth content.
ground_reality: Shigri is PML-N's strong candidate but Shigar's 2020 winner (Amacha/PTI) got 60% of his votes from PTI symbol. Those votes are now scattered — some to PML-N, some to PPP, some abstaining. Shigri's personal vote is maybe 5,000. He needs PTI vote to split evenly to win.

social_media_sentiment: TLP has minimal GB digital presence. Amacha's 2020 PTI win not transferrable to TLP. Mocked in comments.
ground_reality: Amacha's switch from PTI to TLP is bizarre for GB politics. TLP has negligible organization in Baltistan. His 2020 vote was PTI's bat symbol, not personal. He might get 3,500 hardcore religious votes but is not competitive.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Astore campaign visible. Farman Ali has local network. Moderate engagement. But PPP's military-candidate posts getting traction.
ground_reality: GBA-13 is open after Khalid Khurshid's disqualification. PML-N has invested here as 'revenge seat' against PTI. Maj Fahad Hanif Khan (PPP) is strong — military background appeals to Astore's conservative voters. BUT Astore-I leans Sunni/conservative where PML-N/JUI-F traditionally do well. Shahida (Ind/PTI) is female candidate — groundbreaking but faces structural barriers in conservative Astore.

social_media_sentiment: PPP Astore network active. Military background gives Khan credibility. 'Major Fahad' posts shared by patriotic voters. Moderate-high engagement.
ground_reality: Khan's military background is his ace card in conservative Astore. But PPP's 'liberal' brand still hurts him with religious voters. The seat is genuinely competitive — if PPP had fielded a JUI-F type candidate, they'd win. Khan is compromise between PPP ideology and local sensibilities.

social_media_sentiment: Shahida's candidacy as female PTI proxy got initial social media attention but conservative backlash visible in comments. 'Woman in Astore?' negativity.
ground_reality: Female candidates face structural barriers in GB (only 8 women among 403 candidates). Shahida's PTI backing and gender make her a symbol but unlikely to win in conservative Astore-I. However, she may peel 2,500 votes — mostly from progressive youth — that could hurt PPP more than PML-N.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Astore-II campaign strong. Farooq has local roots. High engagement for PML-N posts. No PPP digital presence.
ground_reality: GBA-14 is PML-N's safest seat in Astore. No PPP candidate confirmed yet (per user's data + Tribune report). Shamsul Haq Lone (IPP) won 2020 as PTI but IPP is toxic with PTI base. Farooq benefits from PML-N federal alignment + complete lack of opposition.

social_media_sentiment: IPP has minimal social media presence. Lone seen as 'Gulbar Khan ally' — unpopular with PTI loyalists. Low engagement.
ground_reality: Lone was 2020 winner on PTI symbol. Without it, and with IPP's 'forward bloc' stigma, he's struggling. PML-N's federal patronage flowing to Astore-II. His 4,000 votes come from personal network, not party.

social_media_sentiment: If PPP fields candidate, moderate activity expected. Otherwise independent spoilers.
ground_reality: PPP has not confirmed GBA-14 candidate. This helps PML-N enormously. Any late PPP entry will be too little, too late. Farooq is safe.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Diamer campaign very strong. Wajid has engineering credentials + party backing. High engagement. Religious leaders endorsing PML-N.
ground_reality: Diamer is PML-N's FORTRESS — conservative, Sunni, historically aligned with PML-N/JUI-F. Shah Baig (IPP) won 2020 as Independent but IPP has no Diamer organization. Bashir Ahmed Khan (PPP) is PPP's attempt to break in but Diamer resists PPP's 'liberal' image. Religious leadership (JUI-F aligned) backs PML-N.

social_media_sentiment: Baig has some personal following but IPP brand toxic in Diamer. 'Gulbar Khan ally' label hurts. Low engagement.
ground_reality: Baig won 2020 as Independent with personal vote. Now IPP candidate and current CM Gulbar Khan's ally. But Diamer voters see IPP as Islamabad's puppet. His personal vote may not survive the IPP label. PTI voters in Diamer (2020 national wave) are not loyal to IPP.

social_media_sentiment: PPP Diamer campaign struggling. Khan not well-known digitally. Minimal engagement.
ground_reality: PPP has been trying to penetrate Diamer for decades with limited success. The district's conservative social fabric and Sunni religious leadership align with PML-N/JUI-F, not PPP. Khan is running to show presence, not to win.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Diamer-II campaign strong. Anwar has local name recognition. Moderate-high engagement. Religious network support.
ground_reality: Diamer-II follows Diamer-I pattern — PML-N dominant. Attaullah Shah (PPP) has some base but not enough. Syed Abid Iqbal (Ind/PTI) gets residual PTI vote but Diamer was NEVER PTI's stronghold (PTI won here in 2020 due to national wave, not local strength).

social_media_sentiment: Standard PPP Diamer activity. Shah has religious family background but PPP label limits appeal.
ground_reality: Shah is PPP's compromise candidate in Diamer-II. The district's conservative voters see PPP as 'anti-religious' due to Bhutto legacy. Shah's personal piety helps marginally but cannot overcome structural PML-N advantage.

social_media_sentiment: PTI Diamer support minimal. Iqbal not prominent online.
ground_reality: Iqbal is PTI's proxy but Diamer's PTI vote was always soft — driven by 2020 national sentiment, not local organization. Without bat symbol, PTI is irrelevant in Diamer-II.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Diamer-III campaign visible. Zaman has professional credibility. But Haji Kaman's 'local son' posts getting organic shares.
ground_reality: Diamer-III is competitive. Haji Kaman (Ind/PTI) has personal vote from 2020 and 'Haji' title carries religious weight in conservative Diamer. PTI's persecution narrative resonates with anti-establishment Diamer voters. Muhammad Naseem (PPP) is non-factor. If PTI had bat symbol, this would be toss-up.

social_media_sentiment: Haji Kaman has strong local social media following. PTI base highly mobilized. 'Haji' title + anti-establishment narrative = potent combination in Diamer.
ground_reality: Kaman is a respected local figure. 'Haji' title matters enormously in religious Diamer. PTI's persecution narrative ('state using every trick') resonates with Diamer's independent streak. Without PTI symbol, he's handicapped but personal vote + religious credibility keeps him close.

social_media_sentiment: PPP Diamer-III campaign low-key. Naseem not well-known digitally.
ground_reality: PPP's Diamer problem is structural. Naseem is running because party needs presence, but victory is unrealistic. His votes come from government employees and minority communities.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Diamer-IV campaign very strong. Kifayat has Malik biraderi support. High engagement. Anti-CM sentiment visible in comments.
ground_reality: CORRECTION from initial model: CM Gulbar Khan is NOT popular in his own constituency. He took office through 'forward bloc' horse-trading, not election. Diamer-IV voters see him as Islamabad's puppet. Malik Kifayat is PML-N's strongest Diamer candidate with genuine biraderi network. CM patronage helps but 'CM fatigue' + anti-IPP sentiment hurts Gulbar. PTI's Ayoub Qureshi peels 3,000 votes, helping Kifayat.

social_media_sentiment: State machinery posts for Gulbar visible but organic engagement low. 'Development works' narrative pushed heavily but comments skeptical. 'Where were you before election?' backlash.
ground_reality: Gulbar Khan is incumbent CM with development fund control. But in GB politics, CM patronage is resented as much as appreciated — voters know funds come with strings. His IPP label is toxic. PML-N's Kifayat has federal alignment + local biraderi + anti-CM sentiment. This flips to PML-N.

social_media_sentiment: PTI Diamer-IV support fragmented. Qureshi not prominent. But anti-establishment sentiment helps him.
ground_reality: Qureshi is PTI's proxy. Diamer-IV is two-horse race between Kifayat and Gulbar. Qureshi's 3,000 votes come from PTI loyalists who hate both PML-N and IPP. He doesn't win but ensures Gulbar loses.

social_media_sentiment: PPP Ghizer campaign steady. Syed family name helps. Moderate engagement. BNF-Naji content also visible — nationalist posts getting shares.
ground_reality: CRITICAL VARIABLE: Nawaz Khan Naji (BNF) won GBA-19 in 2020 and is resurgent per ICPS analysis. If Naji enters as Independent/BNF, he becomes #1 and disrupts everything. Based on user's confirmed data (Naji not listed), Shah leads. But this is HIGH UNCERTAINTY. Ghizer has strong nationalist streak.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Ghizer-I campaign moderate. Shadamkhel has some local base.
ground_reality: Shadamkhel is PML-N's attempt to build in Ghizer but the district has strong nationalist (BNF) and PPP tendencies. Without Naji, PML-N might edge PPP, but Syed family's religious following keeps PPP competitive.

social_media_sentiment: PTI Ghizer support exists but Khan is not well-known. Low engagement.
ground_reality: Ghizer-I was never PTI's seat — it was BNF's. Sher Azeem Khan is PTI's proxy but lacks local roots. PTI's 2020 vote here was anti-Naji, not pro-PTI. Without bat symbol, he's not competitive.

social_media_sentiment: High engagement for Nazir Ahmed. Incumbent who joined PPP in April 2026. 'Winner's aura' visible. Youth wing very active.
ground_reality: Nazir Ahmed won GBA-20 in 2020 as Independent and joined PPP in April 2026. He has BOTH personal vote + PPP organization. This is very strong position. Abdul Jahan (PML-N) is respectable but cannot overcome Nazir's dual advantage. Bilawal's Ghizer visit would cement this.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Ghizer-II campaign moderate. Jahan has local name but Nazir's incumbency overshadows.
ground_reality: Jahan is PML-N's standard-bearer but Ghizer-II voters already chose Nazir in 2020. Without compelling reason to switch, incumbency prevails. PML-N's federal alignment helps marginally but not enough.

social_media_sentiment: PTI Ghizer-II support minimal. Raffique not known digitally.
ground_reality: Raffique is PTI's proxy but Ghizer-II has no PTI organizational history. Nazir Ahmed absorbed the independent vote in 2020; Raffique has nothing to build on.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Ghizer-III campaign strong. Ghulam Muhammad is incumbent (won 2020 as PML-N). High engagement.
ground_reality: Ghulam Muhammad won GBA-21 in 2020 as PML-N and is seeking re-election. Strong personal vote + PML-N ticket + federal alignment = very safe. Muhammad Ayub Shah (PPP) has some Syed family support but Ghizer-III is PML-N territory.

social_media_sentiment: PPP Ghizer-III standard activity. Shah has religious family following but limited political base.
ground_reality: Shah is PPP's attempt to challenge in PML-N's Ghizer-III fortress. Syed name helps with religious voters but Ghizer-III voters prioritize development delivery, where incumbent Ghulam Muhammad has advantage.

social_media_sentiment: PTI Ghizer-III support fragmented. Jehanzeb not prominent online.
ground_reality: Jehanzeb is PTI's proxy but PML-N's 2020 win here shows the seat's alignment. Without bat symbol, PTI is irrelevant in Ghizer-III.

social_media_sentiment: PPP Ghanche campaign very active. Dr Hussain's professional credibility posts widely shared. 'Doctor vs. turncoat' narrative emerging. Youth engagement high.
ground_reality: CORRECTION from initial model: Mushtaq Hussain (IPP) won 2020 as Independent but his switch to IPP is toxic in Ghanche. PTI loyalists see IPP as 'Gulbar Khan's party.' Dr Ashiq Hussain (PPP) has been campaigning for 12 months and built medical community network. Muhammad Ibrahim Sanai (PML-N) splits anti-PPP vote. Ghanche-I voters remember Mushtaq as 'independent hero' — IPP label destroys that brand.

social_media_sentiment: Moderate engagement for Mushtaq but 'IPP' comments negative. 'Local son' narrative undermined by party switch.
ground_reality: Mushtaq's personal vote is sticky but IPP label peels 20-30% of his 2020 supporters. PTI voters who backed him as Independent now see him as traitor. PPP's Dr Ashiq benefits from this collapse. PML-N's Sanai peels another chunk.

social_media_sentiment: PML-N Ghanche-I campaign low-key. Sanai has some local base.
ground_reality: Sanai is PML-N's candidate but Ghanche-I has never been PML-N's seat. His role is to build PML-N presence and help Dr Ashiq by splitting Mushtaq's vote.

social_media_sentiment: Seth Anwar has local business community following. PML-N support gives him machinery. But PPP's Amina Bibi getting attention as female candidate.
ground_reality: GBA-23 is messy four-way contest. Seth Anwar has PML-N support + business community. Abdur Raheem (Ind/PTI) gets PTI base. Amina Bibi (PPP) and Rehmat Ullah (JIP) both credible. Fragmentation helps Anwar but any one candidate dropping out changes everything.

social_media_sentiment: PTI Ghanche-II base active. Raheem has some local following. Anti-establishment posts shared.
ground_reality: Raheem is PTI's proxy but Ghanche-II's PTI vote is not dominant. Without bat symbol, he's struggling. But PTI's persecution narrative mobilizes core voters. Amina Bibi (PPP) and Rehmat Ullah (JIP) peel votes, helping Anwar.

social_media_sentiment: Amina Bibi's female candidacy got initial attention. Progressive voters sharing her posts. But conservative backlash in comments.
ground_reality: Amina Bibi is PPP's wildcard. If she consolidates women's vote + progressive youth, she could flip to #2. But conservative Ghanche society makes this difficult. JIP's Rehmat Ullah is also strong — could take #3 instead.

social_media_sentiment: PPP Ghanche-III campaign building. Ismail has engineering background. Moderate engagement. ELECTION DELAYED TO NOV 15.
ground_reality: GBA-24 election postponed to Nov 15, 2026. This gives PPP more time to organize and Bilawal to visit. Dr Asad Shafiq (Ind/PML-N supported) has medical credibility but PPP's Ismail has party organization. Delay helps established parties, hurts PTI momentum.

social_media_sentiment: Shafiq has professional reputation. PML-N support gives resources. Moderate engagement.
ground_reality: Shafiq is PML-N's proxy in GBA-24. As a doctor, he has community respect. But PPP's Ismail has party organization + extra 5 months to campaign. The November delay means federal government's influence could increase, but PPP's ground game improves too.

social_media_sentiment: PTI Ghanche-III support exists but Hussain not prominent. Low engagement.
ground_reality: Hussain is PTI's proxy but GBA-24 has no PTI organizational history. The November delay hurts PTI as other parties get more time to consolidate. PTI's momentum from June campaign will fade by November.
